ai_consensus.gemini:
**Reasoning:** 13 active timers but 0 projected calls means your analysis tool is blind to dynamic triggers. "Hard-coded" daily limits imply local memory variables, which reset upon process restarts.

**(a) Runaway Loop:** If the bot crashes and auto-restarts on errors, local in-memory caps reset to zero. Anthropic's "60s error cooldown" suggests existing retry logic that will infinite-loop across restarts.
**(b) Missing Caps:** No centralized, persistent state (e.g., Redis) or provider-level billing caps exist. Caps are siloed in individual files.
**(c) 2x Rate Blow-up:** If Perplexity hits 2x its cap via restart bypasses, history (the "Apr 23 $100 incident") guarantees a massive spike.
**(d) Unseen Burn:** Gemini ("deep-verify"). The 0-call projection means operators have zero visibility

ai_consensus.grok:
**Risks:**  
(a) Runaway loops: any of the 13 unmapped timers with broken 60s cooldown or retry logic can hammer Anthropic/Haiku in <1 min.  
(b) Missing caps: only 4 services are capped; any direct SDK calls or new helpers bypass shared_gates.py entirely.  
(c) 2× rate on zero baseline = still zero projected cost, so model gives false safety.  
(d) **Anthropic** is most likely to silently burn: ad-hoc reasoning calls sit outside the “deep-verify” path the Gemini cap was sized for and lack per-call visibility.

Map the 13 timers before go-live. Current “LOW” rating is misleading.
